cmd.metadata.groupMode: XBSL: tree grouping (by class / by subsystem)
config.linter.run: When to run the xbsl linter for .xbsl.
config.linter.run.enum.0: Lint as you type (debounced) and on save
config.linter.run.enum.1: Lint only on save and open
config.linter.run.enum.2: No automatic linting (only the "XBSL: check the whole project" command)
config.linter.command: Linter executable (on PATH or an absolute path). Ignored when xbsl.linter.pythonPath is set.
config.linter.pythonPath: Path to a Python interpreter. When set, the linter is invoked as "<python> -m xbsl" (the xbsl package must be installed into that interpreter).
config.linter.dataDir: The Element data root (a directory with index.json). Empty – the linter resolves it itself (the XBSL_DATA_DIR env, an external data package, the bundle).
config.linter.lang.enum.0: As the linter resolves it (env / system locale / ru)
config.linter.select: Check only these rules (comma-separated: id, group or tier letter A/B/C/D). Empty – the default set.
config.linter.ignore: Exclude these rules (comma-separated: id, group or tier letter).
config.linter.debounce: Delay in milliseconds before linting as you type (the onType mode).
config.workspaceLint: On every save of a .xbsl/.yaml file run the full linter pass over the workspace folder and lay the findings over all files. Brings the cross-file rules (code/unknown-type, yaml/unknown-type and others) that a single-buffer check cannot see.
config.workspaceLintTimeout: Timeout of the full workspace run in milliseconds (0 – no limit). On expiry the linter process is stopped and a message goes to the XBSL log.
config.navigation.enabled: Go to definition and completion over the project index (the "xbsl --index" command). If the installed linter does not support the index, navigation just stays silent.
config.projectRoot: Sources root for project-wide runs and the navigation index: a path relative to the workspace folder (or absolute). Empty – the whole workspace folder. Set it when the repository holds examples or copies next to the project: otherwise project-scope rules (Ид uniqueness and others) cross-fire between directories.
config.baseline: Baseline file with the excluded findings (absolute or relative to the workspace folder). Empty - .xbsllint-baseline in the workspace folder when it exists. Excluded findings are suppressed in every run; the lightbulb action "Exclude the finding" records the identity with its reason here.
config.templates.file: File with your own code templates (absolute or relative to the workspace folder). Empty - .xbsl-templates.json in the workspace folder. It extends the builtin set; a template with the same name replaces the builtin one. The format is the one 1C:EDT exports; the templates themselves are XBSL's.
config.lsp.enabled: Work through the long-living xbsl-lsp server (needs the engine with the [lsp] extra: pip install "xbsl[lsp]"). On by default: brings hover, type-aware completion and instant diagnostics; when the server is unavailable the extension quietly keeps working in the regular mode (CLI). Toggling requires a window reload.
config.lsp.command: The LSP server launch command. Empty – xbsl-lsp from PATH, or `<xbsl.linter.pythonPath> -m xbsl.lsp` when the interpreter is set. Example: C:/Python/Scripts/xbsl-lsp.exe.
config.rules: Per-rule overrides. The key is a rule id (`whitespace/trailing`) or a whole group (`style`); the value is off | error | warning | info | hint. "off" hides the findings and skips the rule, a level replaces the rule's own severity; an exact id beats its group, and any key here beats the group dropdowns (`xbsl.groups.*`). The quickest way here is the "Configure rule..." action on any finding.
config.deploy.elemctlPath: The elemctl executable (on PATH or an absolute path). The "XBSL: deploy the project" command runs "elemctl deploy" – build from sources, upload, apply, restart, and verification that the apply actually took effect – as a terminal task.
config.deploy.envFile: A .env file with the connection and the target (ELEMENT_BASE_URL, ELEMENT_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, ELEMENT_APP_ID, ELEMENT_PROJECT_ID), passed to elemctl as --env-file; a path relative to the workspace folder or absolute. Empty – elemctl picks up the .env of the workspace folder itself. Set it e.g. in a git worktree whose .env lives in the main checkout.
config.deploy.appId: The application to deploy to (--app-id). Empty – ELEMENT_APP_ID from the environment / .env file.
config.deploy.extraArgs: Extra arguments appended to "elemctl deploy", separated by spaces (values with spaces are not supported). Example: --project-id 123 --build-version 1.0-42.
config.groups.enum.default: The rules' own levels (the group is on)
config.groups.enum.error: Report every finding of the group as an error
config.groups.enum.warning: Report every finding of the group as a warning
config.groups.enum.info: Report every finding of the group as information
config.groups.enum.hint: Report every finding of the group as a hint
config.groups.enum.off: Disable the group: the rules are skipped, the findings are hidden
config.groups.code: XBSL code: brackets and blocks balance, unused locals and methods, unknown types and enum values, a server call from a client handler, and other code errors. Defaults: 4 rules at error, 12 at warning.
config.groups.yaml: Element descriptions (.yaml): Ид validity and uniqueness, unknown types and properties, missing subsystem imports, dynamic list fields, and other description errors. Defaults: 3 rules at error, 9 at warning, 1 at info.
config.groups.style: The platform code style conventions: naming, types and signatures, collection literals, string interpolation, wrapping and checks. Defaults: 14 rules at warning, 7 at info.
config.groups.typography: Typography in comments and code: em dash, the ellipsis character, curly quotes and guillemets. Defaults: 2 rules at warning, 2 at info.
config.groups.whitespace: Whitespace: trailing spaces and mixed newlines. Default: warning.
config.groups.security: Secrets in the sources: a key or a password as a literal. Default: error.
config.groups.structure: Source structure: the Name.yaml + Name.xbsl pair. Default: warning.
config.groups.form: Forms: a yaml Обработчик: must point to an existing method. Default: warning.
config.groups.query: Query DSL checks (the Query{...} blocks): tables of FROM/JOIN against the project objects.
config.groups.naming: Names of project elements per the 1C:Element standard: number by kind (catalogs plural, enumerations singular), the letter ё and underscores, abbreviations, the kind inside a name, an environment suffix on common modules, the presentation. Defaults: 12 rules at warning.
config.groups.project: Project properties per the 1C:Element standard: the vendor and the name as identifiers starting with a capital letter, a filled-in presentation and vendor presentation, and a three-number version A.B.C. Defaults: 3 rules at warning.
